global.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak a global health emergency
global.summary: The World Health Organization has declared the Ebola outbreak in Central Africa a global health emergency as deaths rise and vaccine shortages deepen concern.
global.whyItMatters: This is a major global health crisis that invites prayer, informed compassion, and attention to how churches respond to fear and suffering.

global.title: No major breakthroughs in Trump-Xi summit, UN chief says - Reuters
global.summary: The Trump-Xi summit in Beijing produced no major breakthroughs, according to the UN secretary-general, leaving trade and geopolitical tensions unresolved.
global.whyItMatters: Global instability shapes economic anxiety, public mood, and pastoral conversations about peace, power, and hope.

global.title: CNBC Daily Open: Bond markets and the Senate rebuke Trump as Iran war worries linger - CNBC
global.summary: Markets fell again as bond yields climbed and concern grew over the Iran conflict, while the U.S. Senate advanced a resolution opposing further military action.
global.whyItMatters: War fears and economic pressure affect families directly and give pastors context for preaching on peace, anxiety, and trust.

canadian.title: US pauses joint defense effort with Canada that dates to WWII - AP News
canadian.summary: The U.S. has paused a longstanding joint defense effort with Canada, raising fresh questions about bilateral security cooperation.
canadian.whyItMatters: Cross-border defense and political trust matter for Canadians, and shifts like this can shape national confidence and public conversation.

canadian.title: Canada’s Public Broadcaster Called Out Over Show That Targeted Conservatives
canadian.summary: CBC is facing backlash over a satirical project accused of using deception to draw in conservative voices for filmed interviews about residential schools.
canadian.whyItMatters: This touches truth, trust, media ethics, and the still-sensitive legacy of residential schools—issues pastors in Canada cannot ignore.

persecutedChurch.title: Chad’s lay faithful join bishops in condemning rising violence, insecurity - Catholic World Report
persecutedChurch.summary: Catholic leaders and lay believers in Chad have publicly condemned worsening insecurity after Boko Haram attacks killed soldiers and deepened national fear.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: It offers a concrete prayer focus for the global church and a reminder of the courage required of believers under threat.

persecutedChurch.title: Harassing Christians undermines both Israel and Jewish history - opinion
persecutedChurch.summary: A Jerusalem Post opinion piece warns that harassment and abuse of Christians in Israel damages the rule of law, public witness, and moral credibility.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: Pastors should be aware of pressures facing Christian communities in the Holy Land and pray for justice, protection, and peace.

leadership.title: Measuring presence instead of impact breaks leadership pipelines - HR Magazine
leadership.summary: This piece argues that healthy leadership cultures reward outcomes, trust, and sustainability rather than constant visibility or overwork.
leadership.whyItMatters: A pastor leading staff and volunteers can model sustainable service and protect people from burnout by valuing fruitfulness over mere availability.

goodNews.title: Reel Hope: Meet Wally - CBS News
goodNews.summary: CBS highlights Wally through the Reel Hope Project, sharing the story of a child in the foster system and keeping vulnerable kids in public view.
goodNews.whyItMatters: It is a gentle reminder of the church’s calling to care for children, foster families, and those who need belonging.
